name: Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal desde Calcena — trail running circular por Penas del Cabo
name_original: Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristóbal desde Calcena — trail running circular por Peñas del Cabo
region_intro: Jogging routes around Calcena offer diverse landscapes in the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park. The region features varied terrain with mountains, barrancos, and gorges, providing scenic views. Runners can explore paths along the Isuela River and through dramatic Valcongosto Gorges. The area is characterized by a unique geological backdrop of pinnacles and crags, making it suitable for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Calcena The most popular jogging route is Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal desde Calcena — trail running circular por Penas del Cabo, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route offers impressive views of the "hidden face of Moncayo." Another top favourite among local runners is Cueva de Añon loop from Añón de Moncayo, a difficult 14.3 miles (23.1 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Valdeherrera Gorge – El Boticario Spring loop from Añón de Moncayo, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through gorges and natural springs, often completed in about 6 hours 46 minutes. Jogging around Calcena is defined by mountain trails, barrancos, and river valleys, with a network that offers options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Calcena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Calcena?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;over 10&lt;/em&gt; dedicated running routes around Calcena listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for trail runners. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Moncayo Natural Park foothills.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Calcena?
A: While many routes in the Calcena area are considered challenging, there is at least one moderate option available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Añón de Moncayo – Cuevas de Añón loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.57 km (5.9 miles) path, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. The region research also mentions accessible paths suitable for various fitness levels, such as the Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop, which offers up to 1,000 ft of elevation gain over a maximum of two hours.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails near Calcena?
A: The running trails around Calcena offer incredibly diverse terrain. You'll encounter mountain paths, deep barrancos (ravines), and picturesque gorges like the Valcongosto Gorges. Many routes also follow riverbanks, such as along the Isuela River, and feature unique geological formations like pinnacles and crags, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Calcena area?
A: Yes, Calcena offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983193214/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-desde-calcena-trail-running-circular-por-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal desde Calcena — trail running circular por Penas del Cabo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.5 km (5.3 miles) long and provides impressive views of the 'hidden face of Moncayo'. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Añón de Moncayo – Cuevas de Añón loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Calcena?
A: Calcena's running routes are rich with natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy panoramic views from viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, or even pass by summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Morrón Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features barrancos, gorges, and the tranquil Isuela River.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Calcena is known for its challenging terrain, with 9 out of 10 routes listed as difficult. For experienced trail runners, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeherrera Gorge – El Boticario Spring loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 45.5 km (28.3 miles) trail with significant elevation changes, often taking over 6 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Añon loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.1 km (14.3 miles) path with nearly 1,000 meters of ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Calcena?
A: Calcena, nestled in the Moncayo Natural Park, offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, for optimal running conditions, spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures and pleasant weather. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, especially within the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features or springs?
A: Yes, the region's topography includes several water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeherrera Gorge – El Boticario Spring loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through gorges and past natural springs. Additionally, the town of Calcena itself lies on the left bank of the Isuela River, offering routes along the water.

Q: Can I find information about the local environment and geology in Calcena?
A: Yes, the Moncayo Natural Park Interpretation Center is located in Calcena. This center provides valuable information about the local geomorphology, flora, and fauna, offering context and further exploration opportunities for those enjoying the natural environment during their runs.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running trails around Calcena?
A: The running trails around Calcena vary significantly in distance and duration. You can find shorter runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983193214/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-desde-calcena-trail-running-circular-por-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal desde Calcena&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 8.5 km (5.3 miles) and takes just over an hour. For longer, more challenging runs, options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeherrera Gorge – El Boticario Spring loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; stretch to 45.5 km (28.3 miles) and can take nearly 7 hours.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running routes that offer scenic vistas?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its stunning views, particularly of the 'hidden face' of Moncayo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983193214/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-desde-calcena-trail-running-circular-por-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal desde Calcena&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically highlighted for its impressive views. Other viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; also offer panoramic vistas of the diverse landscape.
tips:
The Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristóbal is located in the municipality of Los Fayos, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the Queiles River Valley and the surrounding area, including the Val reservoir.

The San Cristóbal hermitage, located next to the viewpoint, is a place of great historical and cultural interest. Built in the 17th century, the hermitage is an example of religious architecture in the region. Access to the viewpoint and the hermitage can be made through several hiking routes that run through the area, offering the opportunity to enjoy nature and panoramic views.

The Peñas del Cabo Caves are located on the western cliffs of the Peñas del Cabo, near Calcena, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. These caves are known for their impressive geological structure and speleothems, such as stalactites, stalagmites and flowstones.

One of the most popular caves is the Cueva Honda, which has been used as a shelter for livestock and has housed archaeological remains from the Neolithic period. In addition, the cave is an important refuge for bat colonies during the summer.

The caves offer a unique experience for caving and nature lovers.😊
